perturbation.original_text: As used herein the term "Information" includes any and all technology, information, knowledge or data whether oral, written, in a physical form or otherwise, which is disclosed for the purpose set forth above and which is identified by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure as being proprietary and/or confidential. "Data" includes notes, designs, drawings, documentation, flow charts, writings, specifications, memoranda, computer software and other similar data. To be covered hereunder, Information disclosed in writing must be marked "proprietary", "confidential", or by words of similar import. Information disclosed orally or visually and identified at the time as proprietary or confidential shall be covered hereunder if it is reduced to writing, marked as provided herein, and transmitted to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days after disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: As used herein the term "Information" includes any and all technology, information, knowledge or data whether oral, written, in a physical form or otherwise. "Data" includes notes, designs, drawings, documentation, flow charts, writings, specifications, memoranda, computer software and other similar data. To be covered hereunder, Information disclosed in writing must be marked as instructed by the Disclosing Party. Information disclosed orally or visually and identified at the time shall be covered hereunder if it is reduced to writing, marked as provided herein, and transmitted to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days after disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "which is disclosed for the purpose set forth above and which is identified by the Disclosing Party at the time of disclosure as being proprietary and/or confidential" from the definition of 'Information', it creates uncertainty. This now means that ANY information, regardless of its purpose or whether it's identified as confidential, could be considered 'Information' under the agreement. Also, by removing  "proprietary", "confidential", or by words of similar import" and replacing by "marked as instructed by the disclosing party", it becomes less clear what consistutes the protection of Information, creating and intext contradiction with Section 2, where the Receiving Party agrees to protect the information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees: a. that with respect to Information, a confidential relationship is established between the Parties, and that Receiving Party shall use Information only for the purpose set forth above; b. to treat Information as confidential and to exercise no less care in its protection than Receiving Party uses in protecting its proprietary and confidential Information, or if Receiving Party has established no such standard of care, then Receiving Party shall exercise such care as the Disclosing Party observes to protect Information; <PAGE> c. to restrict dissemination of Information within its organization to employees having a need to know in connection with the purpose of the disclosure set forth above, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the proprietary and confidential nature thereof and that they agree to and observe the requirements of confidentiality set forth herein; d. not to disclose Information to any other party without the written authorization of Disclosing Party; e. not to duplicate Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party; f. to return or destroy Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the Disclosing Party to identify the information as proprietary or confidential at the time of disclosure. It also replaces 'proprietary', 'confidential', or by words of similar import' with 'marked as instructed by the disclosing party', creating less clarity. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, particularly section a and b, which establishes a confidential relationship and requires the Receiving Party to treat the 'Information' as confidential and protect it. If any information, regardless of its identified confidentiality, falls under the definition of 'Information', it creates an inconsistency with the obligations outlined in the Contradicted Text regarding the confidential treatment of that information. Also, the protection of Information becomes less clear since it changes to 'marked as instructed by the disclosing party'.

perturbation.original_text: to return or destroy Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: to return Information which is in written or physical form promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media," the obligation to return/destroy all instances of the 'Information' is weakened. This change reduces the scope of what must be returned. This change makes Section 9 , where is stated that the disclosure of unauthorized information will cause irreparable harm, contradictory, since the receiving party may have copies or reproductions of the information received
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that the unauthorized disclosure or use by the Receiving Party of the Disclosing Party's proprietary and confidential Information will cause irreparable harm and significant injury, the degree of which may be difficult to ascertain. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party will have the right to obtain an immediate injunction enjoining any breach of this Agreement, as well as the right to pursue any and all other rights and remedies available at law or in equity for such a breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the return or destruction of 'Information' along with 'any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media'. The changed text removes the obligation to return or destroy 'any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media,' weakening the protection of confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 9, which acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of the Disclosing Party's confidential information will cause irreparable harm. Because the receiving party is no longer explicitly obligated to return or destroy all copies, the risk of unauthorized disclosure and subsequent irreparable harm is significantly increased, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to Article 3 hereof, each Party receiving Information under the Agreement shall hold Information in confidence in accordance with the terms of the Agreement for a period of two (2) years from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party receiving Information under the Agreement shall hold Information in confidence for a period of two (2) years from the date of receipt thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "in accordance with the terms of the Agreement", the clause loses its context and makes it difficult to enforce it. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 2, where states 'The Receiving Party agrees' that the receiving party must respect and protect the information, since with the change in Section 4, it's not specified how the information must be protected or in which way it's protection must be in accordance with the terms of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees: a. that with respect to Information, a confidential relationship is established between the Parties, and that Receiving Party shall use Information only for the purpose set forth above; b. to treat Information as confidential and to exercise no less care in its protection than Receiving Party uses in protecting its proprietary and confidential Information, or if Receiving Party has established no such standard of care, then Receiving Party shall exercise such care as the Disclosing Party observes to protect Information; <PAGE> c. to restrict dissemination of Information within its organization to employees having a need to know in connection with the purpose of the disclosure set forth above, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the proprietary and confidential nature thereof and that they agree to and observe the requirements of confidentiality set forth herein; d. not to disclose Information to any other party without the written authorization of Disclosing Party; e. not to duplicate Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party; f. to return or destroy Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires adherence to Article 3 regarding how information is held in confidence. The changed text omits this requirement, creating a contradiction. The contradicted text, which is Section 2, explicitly states how the Receiving Party must protect Information. By removing the reference to Article 3, the changed text makes it unclear how the Receiving Party should hold information in confidence, conflicting with the specific requirements detailed in Section 2. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Because of the trade secret subject matter of Entera's business, Disclosee agrees that it will not solicit the services of any of the employees of Entera during the term of this Agreement and for ninety (90) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosee agrees that it will not solicit the services of any of the employees of Entera during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and for ninety (90) days thereafter", this removes the non-solicitation clause immediately after termination of the agreement. This creates a contradiction with section 6, where is states 'The rights and obligations of the Parties hereunder in respect of Information received under and subject to the Agreement shall survive such termination', where the receiving party is no longer obligated to not solicite employee services, after termination of the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is effective until terminated in accordance with this Article. Either Party, upon two (2) days notice in writing to the other Party, may terminate the Agreement with respect to disclosures made thereafter. The rights and obligations of the Parties hereunder in respect of Information received under and subject to the Agreement shall survive such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a 90-day non-solicitation period after the agreement's termination. The changed text removes this 90-day period. Section 6 states that obligations regarding information received under the agreement survive termination. The original text's non-solicitation obligation clearly applies to information received under the agreement. Removing the 90-day period directly contradicts Section 6, as Disclosee is now free to solicit Entera's employees immediately after termination, even concerning information received during the agreement's term, which violates the surviving obligations. This creates a direct conflict making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: No license, express or implied, in Information is granted to the Receiving Party other than to use Information in the manner and to the extent authorized by the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: No license, express or implied, in Information is granted to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "other than to use Information in the manner and to the extent authorized by the Agreement", this implies that the receiving party cannot use the information for any purposes, going against what is established on Section 2, where it states that  'Receiving Party shall use Information only for the purpose set forth above'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees: a. that with respect to Information, a confidential relationship is established between the Parties, and that Receiving Party shall use Information only for the purpose set forth above; b. to treat Information as confidential and to exercise no less care in its protection than Receiving Party uses in protecting its proprietary and confidential Information, or if Receiving Party has established no such standard of care, then Receiving Party shall exercise such care as the Disclosing Party observes to protect Information; <PAGE> c. to restrict dissemination of Information within its organization to employees having a need to know in connection with the purpose of the disclosure set forth above, and to ensure that such employees are informed of the proprietary and confidential nature thereof and that they agree to and observe the requirements of confidentiality set forth herein; d. not to disclose Information to any other party without the written authorization of Disclosing Party; e. not to duplicate Information without the express written permission of Disclosing Party; f. to return or destroy Information which is in written or physical form, together with any and all copies, negatives, or reproductions or derivative works in any media, promptly upon request of Disclosing Party or upon termination or completion of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Receiving Party to use Information in the manner and to the extent authorized by the Agreement. The changed text removes this authorization, implying the Receiving Party has no right to use the information at all. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' section which states the 'Receiving Party shall use Information only for the purpose set forth above.' The removal of the explicit permission to use the information creates a strong contradiction, as the Receiving Party is now seemingly prohibited from using the information for any purpose, in direct conflict with the section that mandates its use for a specific purpose.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be modified or amended only by a written document duly executed by authorized representatives of Entera and .
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "and may be modified or amended only by a written document duly executed by authorized representatives of Entera and", it does not mention anything about modifying the agreement in the future, creating an in-text contradiction with Section 6, where it talks about agreement termination, but not how it will be re-negotiated and what parts should be modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement is effective until terminated in accordance with this Article. Either Party, upon two (2) days notice in writing to the other Party, may terminate the Agreement with respect to disclosures made thereafter. The rights and obligations of the Parties hereunder in respect of Information received under and subject to the Agreement shall survive such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for modifications or amendments via a written document. The changed text removes this possibility. Section 6 discusses agreement termination, implying the possibility of renegotiation or modification in the future. By removing the explicit mechanism for modification, the changed text contradicts the implied possibility of modification when the contract is terminated and re-negotiated.
